glossary.subtitle: Key terms and concepts explained
glossary.definition.ADR: Architecture Decision Record – a short document that captures an important architectural decision, its context, and trade-offs.
glossary.definition.nanoassembler: A hypothetical molecular machine capable of building complex structures atom-by-atom with atomic precision.
glossary.definition.starship: SpaceX’s fully reusable spacecraft designed for interplanetary travel and rapid Earth-to-Earth transport.
glossary.definition.optimus: Tesla’s humanoid robot designed to perform useful tasks in factories, homes, and beyond.
glossary.definition.neuralink: Brain-computer interface technology that aims to enable direct communication between the human brain and computers.
glossary.definition.fusion: A nuclear reaction in which two light atomic nuclei combine to form a heavier nucleus, releasing enormous energy.
glossary.definition.terraforming: The hypothetical process of deliberately modifying a planet’s atmosphere, temperature, and ecology to make it habitable for humans.
title: Architecture Decision Records
welcome: Welcome to the Architecture Decision Records of the R&D Center. Here you can find Architecture Decision Records (ADR) of the project.
definition-description: An Architecture Decision is a design choice that addresses a functional or non-functional requirement that is architecturally significant. An Architecture Decision Record (ADR) describes a single Architecture Decision; the collection of ADRs created and maintained in a project constitutes its decision log.
definition-description-2: You can become familiar with the design of the project by reading its decision log in chronological order. Moreover, maintaining this documentation aims at:
purpose-1: Making reasoning about design easily accessible
purpose-3: Improving and speeding up the onboarding of new team members
purpose-4: Avoiding blind acceptance/reversal of a past decision
purpose-5: Formalizing the decision process of the team
purpose-6: Serving as a valuable input for AI for automation purposes
